methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
MightyFly is a Series A stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The company has raised $10M to date. The comp universe consists of eight private companies in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker; no direct public comparables exist in this narrow category.

Stage-Progression valuation is the applicable method here. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression, this method implies a $20M valuation. Peer-Median comparison is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints within the narrow peer set; while four same-sector peers exist, filtering for stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M to $23M, with a median of $20M, derived from single-method triangulation. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer valuation data at MightyFly's stage and the reliance on a standardized multiplier that may not reflect company-specific risk or traction metrics. Additional diligence on unit economics, technology differentiation, and capital efficiency would strengthen conviction in final pricing within this range.
